learning.title: Live Podcast Production
learning.description: Live audio/video production for podcasts and streams

obsessing.title: Color Theory
obsessing.description: Studying how color drives decisions in interfaces and film

building.title: PointPal
building.description: AI travel platform optimizing credit card points for cheaper flights

output: > Seeking: Design studios, startups, product companies
> Location: San Francisco, CA (open to remote)
> Available: Yes, always down for coffee
> Shoot me an email to schedule:
Nice try, but you can't leave that easily...
> This terminal is your home now.

reading.title: Man and His Symbols
reading.author: Carl Jung
reading.description: Jung hands you a lantern and points it inward—mapping the archetypes, symbols, and dream logic that shape behavior beneath the surface of consciousness.

reading.title: The Book of Five Rings
reading.author: Miyamoto Musashi
reading.description: A swordsman who never lost writes in the days before his death—on timing, terrain, rhythm, and the discipline of clearing the mind so the body can act without hesitation.

reading.title: Grid Systems in Graphic Design
reading.author: Josef Müller-Brockmann
reading.description: The book that taught a generation of designers that the grid is not a cage—it is the skeleton beneath everything beautiful, a system of columns, margins, and whitespace that turns information into visual language.

reading.title: Autobiography of a Yogi
reading.author: Paramahansa Yogananda
reading.description: A yogi walks between worlds—recounting levitating saints, divine encounters, and decades of Kriya Yoga practice with the quiet insistence that consciousness is the only frontier worth exploring.

reading.title: Barbarians at the Gate
reading.author: Bryan Burrough & John Helyar
reading.description: Grown men in suits destroy a $25 billion company over pride and leverage—the story of the RJR Nabisco buyout, where egos wrote checks that gutted employees and made bankers billionaires.

reading.title: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?
reading.author: Philip K. Dick
reading.description: In the ash of a post-nuclear world, a bounty hunter tracks down androids using an empathy test—and the closer he looks, the less certain he becomes about which side of the line he stands on.

reading.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra
reading.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
reading.description: A prophet descends from the mountain with fire in his mouth—preaching the Übermensch, the death of God, and eternal recurrence: the demand that you love your life so completely you'd choose to live it again, unchanged, forever.

reading.title: Neuromancer
reading.author: William Gibson
reading.description: Before the internet had a name, Gibson dreamed it as neon and rot—a burned-out console cowboy hired for one last heist, jacking into a vast consensual hallucination controlled by rival AIs and corporate dynasties.

reading.title: The Masnavi, Book One
reading.author: Rumi
reading.description: A reed cut from its bed cries for reunion—and inside that cry, Rumi unfolds 25,000 verses on divine love, the ego's illusions, and the Sufi path from separation back to the source.

reading.title: Ender's Game
reading.author: Orson Scott Card
reading.description: A boy genius is isolated, accelerated, and shaped into the perfect military commander through brutal simulations—then sent to fight humanity's final war against an alien civilization, never told the games were never games.

reading.title: Atlas Shrugged
reading.author: Ayn Rand
reading.description: The engines of the world go quiet one by one as its greatest minds vanish—Rand's 1,000-page case for rational self-interest, individual achievement, and the catastrophe of a society that punishes both.

reading.author: George Orwell
reading.description: A man tries to hold a single true thought inside a totalitarian state built on surveillance, rewritten history, and doublethink—and the horror is how slowly, how warmly, the system breaks him.

reading.title: The Prophet
reading.author: Kahlil Gibran
reading.description: A prophet prepares to leave the city he has called home for twelve years, and the people ask him everything—on love, children, work, pain, freedom, death—and his answers arrive like light through a cracked door.

reading.title: The Romanovs
reading.author: Simon Sebag Montefiore
reading.description: Three centuries of god-appointed rulers who murdered their way to the throne and were murdered off it—Montefiore traces the Romanov dynasty from its brutal rise through Peter the Great to the execution of Nicholas II, with opulence, paranoia, and blood on every page.
